title: Lithocarpus milroyi
taxonomy: Lithocarpus milroyi is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to the Himalayas. The classification of Lithocarpus milroyi places it within the genus Lithocarpus, which consists of beeches and chestnuts. The genus Lithocarpus is further classified under the family Fagaceae and the order Fagales. This species is often used in reforestation efforts due to its ability to tolerate a range of environmental conditions.

intro: Lithocarpus milroyi, also known as Milroy's oak or Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus milroyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into formulations such as syrups and capsules.
